versions.title: A smoother connection to Claude
versions.summary: Updating Persona's Claude extension now confirms itself on its own — and the app goes by Persona everywhere it shows up.

highlights.title: The Claude extension update just works
highlights.body: When you update Persona's Claude extension, the panel now flips to “Installed” on its own the moment it's done — no more backing out of Settings and reopening it to see that it went through.

highlights.title: Now just Persona
highlights.body: The app now shows up as “Persona” in Spotlight, the Dock, and search — matching the name it already used inside. Fresh installs pick this up automatically.

versions.title: A brief with room to breathe
versions.summary: The brief now grows as you type and scrolls when it fills up — the same roomy, caption-style box — so you can capture the whole story behind a post.

highlights.title: Room to write your brief
highlights.body: The brief field now grows as you type and starts scrolling once it fills, just like your caption box. Spell out the full intent behind a post — what it's really about, who it's for, the angle you're going for — instead of squeezing it onto a line or two.

highlights.title: More of it in quick capture
highlights.body: When you jot down a quick brief, more of what you wrote now stays visible before it scrolls — so a longer note is readable at a glance without opening the whole post.

versions.title: The Edit page — crop, matte, and split panoramas
versions.summary: Crop, Matte, and Panorama now live together in one Edit sheet — reframe a photo, drop it on a colored background, or split a wide shot into a carousel, all from Mac or iPhone.

highlights.title: One Edit page for everything
highlights.body: Crop, Matte, and Panorama are now tabs in a single Edit sheet instead of separate tools — open it once and switch between them without losing your place. Works the same way on Mac and iPhone.

highlights.title: Matte any photo or video
highlights.body: Fit a photo or video onto a colored background instead of cropping it away — pick your color, dial in the border, and it composes cleanly with a crop underneath. Works for video too, not just photos.

highlights.title: Split a wide photo into a carousel
highlights.body: Turn a panorama into 2 to 6 seamless carousel slides with one tap. Not happy with the split? Revert it losslessly and try again — your original photo is always right there.

highlights.title: More reliable when editing across devices
highlights.body: Edits made on your iPhone now merge correctly with a Mac library that's open at the same time, so a split or a crop never gets silently reverted.

highlights.title: Various bug fixes
highlights.body: Plus fixes for carousel swiping and how panoramas show up in your Library.

versions.title: Meet Persona
versions.summary: Content Strategy is now Persona — the same app and the same library you already have, with a fresh name and a new face for the work you put forward.

highlights.title: A new name, a new face
highlights.body: Content Strategy is now Persona. It's the same app you've been using — every brand, post, and plan is right where you left it — with a new name, a new icon, and a refined identity throughout.

highlights.title: Everything is right where you left it
highlights.body: Nothing moved and nothing changed but the name and the look. All of your brands, posts, media, and schedules are exactly as they were — Persona reads your library the same way it always has.

versions.title: Crop, reframe, and cleaner carousels
versions.summary: A brand-new Crop tool lets you frame every photo exactly how you want on Mac and iPhone, while carousels finally swipe and preview true to shape.

highlights.title: Crop and reframe your photos
highlights.body: A new Crop tool lets you pick a shape and reposition each photo inside it — the full image stays in view while you move and resize the frame right on top of it. Drag to move, use the zoom dial or pinch to tighten, and reopen a crop anytime to pick up right where you left off. Your original files are never changed.

highlights.title: Frame each carousel photo, on any device
highlights.body: Step through a carousel and reframe every photo on its own so the whole set lines up beautifully — on your Mac or right from your iPhone. Choose a standard shape or go Custom to pull the corners into anything you like, and the whole carousel snaps to that same shape to stay uniform.

highlights.title: Cleaner carousel swiping
highlights.body: Swiping through a multi-photo post now snaps neatly from slide to slide with no half-image seams, opens squarely on the first image instead of stalling between two, and the iPhone swipe that used to freeze is smooth again.

highlights.title: True-to-shape previews everywhere
highlights.body: Every carousel slide now shows the exact crop it'll publish with — matching across your Library cards, the post view, and your phone. Tall vertical photos finally show full-height, and posts set to Auto frame the same way on every screen.

highlights.title: Various bug fixes
highlights.body: Plus a handful of smaller fixes and refinements throughout the app.

versions.title: A quiet fix
versions.summary: The Recently Deleted indicator could get stuck on even after there was nothing left to recover. It clears properly now.

versions.title: Recently Deleted, right where you need it
versions.summary: Recovering a deleted post is easier to find, and your phone and iPad just caught up to your Mac's Library.

highlights.title: Recently Deleted moved into the Library toolbar
highlights.body: Instead of a sidebar row, look for the clock icon next to Library's other tools. It lights up the moment something's waiting to be recovered, and now respects whichever brand you're filtering to.

highlights.title: Your phone and iPad caught up
highlights.body: The Library toolbar — filter by status or date, sort newest or oldest, and select multiple posts to delete or change status at once — is now on iPhone and iPad too, along with Recently Deleted for recovering anything you remove.

versions.title: Cleaner syncing between devices
versions.summary: Edits made on different devices at the same time now merge more precisely, and the Claude integration only asks for an update when it actually needs one.

versions.title: A faster way to work your Library
versions.summary: Find and tidy your posts in seconds, and lock in timing right as you compose.

highlights.title: Filter your Library
highlights.body: Narrow your Library to just drafts, scheduled, or published posts, or to a specific date range — and it remembers your choice next time you open up. Everything you've ever published is easy to find again, too.

highlights.title: Handle posts in batches
highlights.body: Select several posts at once to delete them together or set their status in one move, so tidying up takes seconds instead of one post at a time.

highlights.title: Schedule while you compose
highlights.body: Set a post's status right from the clean compose screen — pick Scheduled and a date picker slides in so you can lock the timing up front, no need to open the full editor first.

versions.title: Claude knows your library better
versions.summary: A behind-the-scenes tune-up so Claude understands how your content is organized the moment it connects. Nothing to set up — it just works a little smarter.

highlights.title: Smarter out of the box
highlights.body: When you connect your library to Claude, it now arrives already understanding how things fit together — brands, posts, scheduling, and your creative profile — so it stays on-brand and asks before deleting anything.

versions.title: One-click Claude, everywhere
versions.summary: Connecting your library to Claude is now a single click — and it shows up in both Cowork and Claude Desktop chats.

highlights.title: Connect to Claude in one click
highlights.body: Add your library to Claude in a single click, and it now shows up in both Cowork and regular Claude Desktop chats. From there Claude can work with your content directly — list brands, schedule posts, attach media, and more.

highlights.title: Stays in sync as you update
highlights.body: When a new version moves ahead of your Claude connection, a small marker appears in Settings so you can refresh it in one tap and keep everything talking.

versions.title: A rare crash, quietly closed
versions.summary: A stability fix for a startup hiccup that could affect libraries synced across multiple Macs. If you never hit it, you won't notice a thing — which is exactly the point.

highlights.title: Steadier on launch
highlights.body: Closed a rare case where a duplicate brand record left behind by iCloud sync could stop the app from opening. It now tidies the duplicate on the way up and starts clean.

versions.title: A cleaner, calmer Library
versions.summary: A visual tune-up across the Library and sidebar — tighter, lighter, and more like a proper workstation, so your content does the talking.

highlights.title: A tighter, lighter sidebar
highlights.body: The navigation rail slimmed down — smaller type, less padding, and a quieter “Recently Deleted” tucked at the bottom — so it reads like a focused workspace instead of a heavy menu bar.

highlights.title: Cards that show more at a glance
highlights.body: Library cards are a touch smaller with a cleaner title and quieter status pills, so more of your work fits on screen without the chrome getting in the way.

highlights.title: Landscape and text posts fit right
highlights.body: Wide 16:9 and YouTube-shaped posts now stretch across two columns so they carry real weight, and text-only posts render as wide, tidy cards that read like a social post.

versions.title: A lighter footprint
versions.summary: Another one for the "you'll never notice, and that's the point" pile. We trimmed how much the app writes to disk and how loudly it talks to itself behind the scenes — so it stays lean and leaves your iCloud alone unless something actually changed.

highlights.title: Writes less, syncs cleaner
highlights.body: The app now skips re-saving anything that hasn't truly changed, so it stops giving iCloud reasons to spawn duplicate copies of your posts. Quieter syncing, fewer stray files.

highlights.title: Tidier under the hood
highlights.body: We put a lid on the app's internal diagnostic log and calmed a chatty background process, so things stay light even when iCloud is having a slow day.

versions.title: A quiet tune-up
versions.summary: No new buttons in this one — just the satisfying kind of update. We made things faster and tougher under the hood so the app stays out of your way and you can get back to creating. You don't have to read this one… but we're glad you did.

highlights.title: Bye-bye, beach balls
highlights.body: Photos and thumbnails now load quietly in the background instead of freezing things up while iCloud catches up. Scroll your Library, flip through a carousel — it just keeps pace.

highlights.title: Quietly bulletproofed
highlights.body: We tightened how the app and your AI assistant share your library, so two edits landing at the same moment can never step on each other. You'll never see it work — which is exactly the point.

versions.title: The composer, reimagined
versions.summary: A redesigned composer, three new platforms, text-only posts, and a tap-to-copy caption handoff make this the biggest content release yet.

highlights.title: A roomier place to write
highlights.body: Your caption now sits in a wide column right under your photos and grows as you type, so long captions never feel cramped. The whole composer is cleaner — your media is centered, your title and publishing controls stay tidy on the right, and platform previews stretch across the full width below.

highlights.title: Plan for YouTube, Threads & X
highlights.body: Three new platforms join the lineup — YouTube long-form, Threads, and X — each with its own live preview so you can see exactly how a post will look. Flip on X Premium for any brand that pays for it and your caption counter follows along, allowing up to 25,000 characters. Each brand keeps its own setting.

highlights.title: Post text on its own
highlights.body: A caption is now a complete post all by itself — no photo or video required. Write the words you want to share and schedule or export them like any other post, and they show up in your Library as a clean, phone-style text card instead of a blank placeholder.

highlights.title: Tailor platforms per brand
highlights.body: Each brand can now turn platforms on or off in its Creative Profile, so the composer only ever offers the channels that brand actually posts to. New posts no longer pre-pick a platform either — you decide where each one goes, and disabled buttons tell you exactly what's still needed before you can schedule.

highlights.title: Tap to copy your captions
highlights.body: Exporting a post now gives you one clean caption page you can open right on your phone and tap to copy each platform's caption, hashtags, and first comment — plus a PDF and per-platform text files. A new Copy button in the app grabs any version in a single click, and what you copy is exactly what shows up in your export.

highlights.title: Smarter caption limits
highlights.body: Each platform now counts the caption it will actually post and warns you when it runs too long, even on platforms you never customized. TikTok's limit is corrected too, so you'll know before a video caption runs over.

highlights.title: Move through the app faster
highlights.body: Press Command-N from any screen to jump straight into a fresh post — and if you start something new while a post is still in progress, the app offers to save it as a draft first so your work is never lost. A two-finger swipe right on your trackpad now takes you back out of a post, just like on iPhone and iPad.

highlights.title: Smoother and more polished
highlights.body: Your Library now glides gracefully as cards rearrange when you open or close the sidebar, photos stay neatly in place above the caption, and the occasional spinning beach ball is gone — thumbnails load faster so scrolling and typing stay snappy throughout.

versions.title: What's New, in the app
versions.summary: See exactly what changed in every release — and never get stuck on an old version again.

highlights.title: A What's New window
highlights.body: Browse every release in one place: pick a version on the left, scroll through all the changes on the right. Open it any time from the app menu or Settings.

highlights.title: Updates that just happen
highlights.body: New versions now download quietly in the background. When one's ready, the sidebar shows it with the version number — click to relaunch and you're current.

highlights.title: Always up to date
highlights.body: The app checks for updates far more often now, including when you come back to it — so you're never quietly running an old build.

versions.title: Smarter captions & a calmer title bar

highlights.title: Caption length cautions
highlights.body: A gentle per-platform warning appears when a caption runs past a platform's limit — it never blocks you, it just lets you know.

highlights.title: Scrollable caption editor
highlights.body: Long captions now scroll in place instead of stretching the editor, so the layout stays put while you write.

highlights.title: Title-bar fade
highlights.body: Content now dissolves softly under the title bar as you scroll, keeping the window controls clean and legible.

highlights.title: Remove a single carousel item
highlights.body: Right-click any photo or clip in a carousel to remove just that one — no need to rebuild the whole post.

highlights.title: Click anywhere to dismiss focus
highlights.body: Click empty space in the editor to drop the cursor out of a field, so you're not stuck in a text box.

highlights.title: Redesigned sidebar & title bar
highlights.body: The sidebar sits flush against the window and the title bar is calmer and roomier — less chrome, more content.

highlights.title: Carousel aspect-ratio selector
highlights.body: Preview a carousel in any platform-standard ratio — 4:5, 1:1, 1.91:1, or 9:16 — so you can see how it'll frame before you post.

highlights.title: Masonry library layout
highlights.body: The Library now flows like a Pinterest board, so tall and wide posts sit naturally side by side.

versions.title: A library you can actually see
versions.summary: Your content now lives in a real folder you can open in Finder — and it syncs reliably across your Macs.

highlights.title: Your library is a real folder
highlights.body: Every brand is a folder and every post is a folder with its media inside — browse it in Finder any time, it's yours.

highlights.title: Reliable syncing across Macs
highlights.body: The library now syncs through iCloud Drive, so your two Macs stay in step without the sync trouble of before.
